skill.name: semantic-scholar-database
skill.description: Access the Semantic Scholar Graph API to search papers and retrieve paper/author/citation data when you need literature discovery or citation graph exploration.
skill.summary: Search academic papers, get paper and author details, and explore citation graphs via the Semantic Scholar API.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 53 potential issues across 4 files. All findings are false positives upon evaluation. The 25 external_commands detections are Markdown code formatting backticks mistaken for Ruby shell execution. The 10 network detections are legitimate API calls to api.semanticscholar.org. The 10 env_access detections are standard S2_API_KEY authentication for rate limit improvement. The scripts detection is a standard Python import in example code. No prompt injection attempts, obfuscation, or malicious intent was detected. This is a legitimate, well-documented academic research API client.

low_findings.title: External API Communication to Semantic Scholar
low_findings.description: The skill makes HTTP GET requests to api.semanticscholar.org/graph/v1 for paper search, paper details, author details, and citation traversal. This is expected behavior for a research database API client. All requests target the legitimate Semantic Scholar API endpoint. No data exfiltration risk.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Access for API Authentication
low_findings.description: The skill reads S2_API_KEY from environment variables on import and passes it via x-api-key header to api.semanticscholar.org. This is the standard authentication method documented by the Semantic Scholar API. The key is only transmitted to the legitimate API endpoint. No key exfiltration or unauthorized use was detected.

content.user_title: Search and Explore Academic Papers with Semantic Scholar
content.value_statement: Finding relevant academic papers and understanding citation networks is time-consuming. This skill connects you directly to the Semantic Scholar database, letting you search papers, explore citation graphs, and retrieve author profiles with a single command.
actual_capabilities: Search academic papers by keyword, title, or identifier using the Semantic Scholar Graph API
Retrieve detailed paper metadata including abstract, venue, publication year, and citation count
Fetch author profiles with publication history and h-index information
Traverse citation networks to find papers that cite or are referenced by a target paper
Support higher rate limits through optional S2_API_KEY environment variable
Integrate with Python workflows for automated literature review pipelines
limitations: Requires internet access to connect to the Semantic Scholar API
Rate limited to 100 requests per 5 minutes without an API key
Does not provide full-text PDF downloads of papers
Search results depend on Semantic Scholar database coverage and indexing

use_cases.title: Literature Review for Research Papers
use_cases.description: A graduate student searches for papers on a specific topic, retrieves metadata including abstracts, and maps citation connections to build a comprehensive literature review.

use_cases.title: Author Impact Analysis
use_cases.description: A research evaluation specialist looks up an author profile to examine their publication record, h-index, and institutional affiliations for assessment or collaboration purposes.

use_cases.title: Citation Network Exploration
use_cases.description: A principal investigator discovers related work by traversing citation and reference links from a known paper, identifying influential prior work and follow-up studies.

prompt_templates.title: Search papers by keyword
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for papers about [your research topic], limit to [number] results, and show me titles with publication years.
prompt_templates.scenario: Basic paper discovery for a new research topic

prompt_templates.title: Get detailed paper information
prompt_templates.prompt: Get paper details for Semantic Scholar paper ID [paper ID]. Include the title, abstract, authors, publication venue, and year.
prompt_templates.scenario: Retrieve comprehensive metadata for a known paper

prompt_templates.title: Explore citation relationships
prompt_templates.prompt: For paper [paper ID], find all papers that cite it and all papers it references. Show me the titles and publication years.
prompt_templates.scenario: Citation graph traversal to find related work

prompt_templates.title: Author profile and publication lookup
prompt_templates.prompt: Find author profile for Semantic Scholar author ID [author ID]. Show me their name, affiliations, publication count, and h-index.
prompt_templates.scenario: Author research assessment and impact evaluation

output_examples.input: Search for papers about transformer neural networks, return 3 results.
output_examples.output:
I found 3 papers matching your search:

1. Attention Is All You Need (2017) - Introduces the Transformer architecture, a novel neural network based solely on attention mechanisms.
2. BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers (2019) - Applies Transformer architecture to language understanding tasks.
3. An Image is Worth 16x16 Words: Transformers for Image Recognition (2021) - Adapts Transformer architecture for computer vision tasks.

Paper: Attention Is All You Need (2017)
Venue: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems
Total Citations: Over 95000

This foundational paper introduced the Transformer model architecture, which replaced recurrent neural network layers with multi-head self-attention mechanisms. It has been cited extensively across NLP, computer vision, and multimodal AI research communities.

best_practices: Set the S2_API_KEY environment variable for higher rate limits when performing bulk searches or systematic literature reviews
Use specific search queries with relevant keywords for more accurate and targeted paper discovery results
Combine citation graph traversal with paper details retrieval to build a complete picture of the research landscape
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode API keys in skill configuration files or scripts - always use environment variables
Do not make excessive rapid requests without considering rate limits, which can result in temporary API bans
Do not rely on paper IDs without first verifying they exist in the Semantic Scholar database through a search

faq.question: What is the Semantic Scholar Graph API?
faq.answer: It is a free API provided by the Allen Institute for AI that gives access to a large database of academic papers, authors, and citation relationships.

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: No. You can make requests without a key, but rate limits are lower at 100 requests per 5 minutes.

faq.question: How do I get a Semantic Scholar API key?
faq.answer: Visit the Semantic Scholar API website and register for a free account to receive your API key.

faq.question: Can this skill download full-text PDFs of papers?
faq.answer: No. This skill retrieves paper metadata and citation data only, not full-text PDF files.

faq.question: What types of paper identifiers does this skill support?
faq.answer: Semantic Scholar assigns unique paper IDs. You can also search by keywords, title fragments, or author names.

faq.question: How current is the data from Semantic Scholar?
faq.answer: The Semantic Scholar database is updated regularly. Data freshness depends on the API indexing and update schedule.
